arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i binutils | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using various URL patterns
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Pattern 1: _.debug with elf-buildid-sym
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo " - _.debug/elf-buildid-sym"

# Pattern 2: libmonosgen-2.0.so with elf-buildid-sym  
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo " - libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym"

# Pattern 3: debuginfod style
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo"
echo " - debuginfod style"

# Pattern 4: NuGet/dotnet symbol server
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo " - nuget symbols"

# Pattern 5: Just the binary itself
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo " - libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid"
arguments.description: Try downloading debug symbols from symbol servers

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try downloading debug symbols from symbol servers

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key information is:

1. The library is `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`
2. The frames are at various PC offsets within that library

To symbolicate these frames, I would need:
- The debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with that specific BuildId
- A tool like `llvm-symbolizer`, `addr2line`, or similar

The BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be` identifies the specific build. For .NET runtime libraries, Microsoft publishes symbol packages.


I can try to fetch the symbol file from Microsoft's symbol server using the BuildId and then use a tool like `llvm-symbolizer` to resolve the addresses to function names and source locations. The Microsoft symbol server uses a specific URL format for ELF binaries based on the BuildId. Let me try constructing the correct Microsoft symbol server URL for the ELF binary using the build ID format, testing both the standard and newer sym variants to locate the debug symbols.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i binutils | head -5

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using various URL patterns
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Pattern 1: _.debug with elf-buildid-sym
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo " - _.debug/elf-buildid-sym"

# Pattern 2: libmonosgen-2.0.so with elf-buildid-sym  
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo " - libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym"

# Pattern 3: debuginfod style
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo"
echo " - debuginfod style"

# Pattern 4: NuGet/dotnet symbol server
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo " - nuget symbols"

# Pattern 5: Just the binary itself
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo " - libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid"

arguments.command:
# Symbolicate the libmonosgen-2.0.so frames using addr2line with the debug symbols
# The -f flag shows function names, -C demangles C++ names, -e specifies the binary
ADDRS="0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04"

echo "=== Symbolicated backtrace ==="
echo ""
i=1
for addr in $ADDRS; do
  frame_num=$((i))
  result=$(addr2line -f -C -e /tmp/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg "$addr" 2>&1)
  func=$(echo "$result" | head -1)
  loc=$(echo "$result" | tail -1)
  printf "  #%02d pc %-18s libmonosgen-2.0.so  %s at %s\n" "$frame_num" "$addr" "$func" "$loc"
  i=$((i+1))
done

data.content:
Here's the full symbolicated backtrace for your crash:

```
#00 pc 0x06ae1c  libc.so                abort+164
#01 pc 0x222098  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast          (icall.c:6244)
#02 pc 0x2241ac  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast_raw      (icall-def.h:202)
#03 pc 0x1abc80  libmonosgen-2.0.so     do_icall                                       (interp.c:2457)
#04 pc 0x1abd14  libmonosgen-2.0.so     get_interp_to_native_trampoline                (interp.c:1732)
#05 pc 0x16dfa4  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                  (type-checking.c)
#06 pc 0x16db6c  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                  (type-checking.c:506)
#07 pc 0x16de04  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                  (type-checking.c:528)
#08 pc 0x00307c  <anonymous JIT code>
```

**Summary:** The app called `Environment.FailFast` with the abort message *"not combatible"* (visible in the tombstone). The call originated from the Mono interpreter (`do_icall` → `get_interp_to_native_trampoline`) during a type-checking operation (`handle_isinst`). Frame #08 is JIT-compiled managed code that would require a managed stack trace to resolve further.

Debug symbols were fetched from Microsoft's symbol server using BuildId `1eb39fc7...`.
